channel.title: How, When to Take Vit. D on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in How, When to Take Vit. D on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Taking 100,000 IU of Vitamin D daily to quarterly - review
item.description:
• Has a High Dose of Vitamin D3 Impacted Health Conditions in Older Adults?-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Focusing on Dose 100,000 IU
      • VitaminDWiki - Diseases treated by high-dose Vitamin D - many studies

Has a High Dose of Vitamin D3 Impacted Health Conditions in Older Adults?-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Focusing on Dose 100,000 IU

Nutrients. 2024 Jan 14;16(2):252. doi: 10.3390/nu16020252.

Barbara Owczarek 1 2 3, Anna Ziomkiewicz 4, Edyta Łukowska-Chojnacka 1

‘T = tested group, C = Control group, DB = double blind, OL = open label, RCT = randomized clinical trial, NA = not available,

item.title: 14 Conservative Vitamin D recommendations in Ukraine
item.description:
• Ukrainian Consensus on Diagnosis and Management of Vitamin D Deficiency in Adults

Ukrainian Consensus on Diagnosis and Management of Vitamin D Deficiency in Adults

Nutrients 2024, 16, 270. https://doi.org/10.3390/ nu16020270

Nataliia Grygorieva * , Mykola Tronko 2, Volodymir Kovalenko 3, Serhiy Komisarenko 4 , Tetiana Tatarchuk 5, Ninel Dedukh 1, Mykola Veliky 4®, Serhiy Strafun 6C , Yulia Komisarenko 7C , Andrii Kalashnikov 6 , Valeria Orlenko 2, Volodymyr Pankiv 8 , Oleg Shvets 9 , Inna Gogunska 10 and Svitlana Regeda 11

item.title: Need 2X Vitamin D and other micronutrients if eat 2X more calories
item.description:
• The science of micronutrients in clinical practice – Report on the ESPEN symposium
      • Note: Most farmers and vets know this, but very few doctors

The science of micronutrients in clinical practice – Report on the ESPEN symposium

ESPEN ENDORSED RECOMMENDATION| VOL 43, # 1, P268-283, Jan 2024 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.clnu.2023.12.006

Background & aims

The European Society for Clinical Nutrition and Metabolism published its first clinical guidelines for use of micronutrients (MNs) in 2022. A two-day web symposium was organized in November 2022 discussing how to apply the guidelines in clinical practice. The present paper reports the main findings of this symposium.

item.title: Simple vitamin D supplementation - daily, weekly, or monthly
item.description:
• Supplementing Vitamin D in Different Patient Groups to Reduce Deficiency
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Conference - 6th Minimum, Maximum, Optimum - Sept 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - 13 reasons why many seniors need more vitamin D (both dose and level) - July 2023

Supplementing Vitamin D in Different Patient Groups to Reduce Deficiency

Nutrients 2023, 15(17), 3725; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15173725

item.title: Vitamin D daily - Evolution: 4,000 IU, Government: 800 IU– Veith
item.description:
• Critique of Public Health Guidance for Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in the Context of Cancer and COVID-19
      • More than 4,000 IU or a different form of Vitamin D is need if have poor digestion
      • Those who are much lower weight (children) often can reach 30 ng with much lower doses
      • Data from 106 dosing trials indicates that 7,000 IU is needed to get 97.5% >30 ng

Critique of Public Health Guidance for Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in the Context of Cancer and COVID-19

Anticancer Research 42: 5027-5034 (2022) doi: 10.21873/anticanres .16011

item.title: Vitamin D: not one size, type, form, route for all
item.description:
• Vitamin D: Dosing, levels, form, and route of administration: Does one approach fit all?
      • Study says vitamin D needed varies with
      • Vitamin D IU needed varies with
      • VitaminDWiki - Personalized treatment of Vitamin D

Vitamin D: Dosing, levels, form, and route of administration: Does one approach fit all?

Reviews in Endocrine and Metabolic Disorders (2021) 22:1201-1218 https://doi.org/10.1007/s11154-021-09693-7

John P. Bilezikian • Anna Maria Formenti • Robert A. Adler • Neil Binkley • Roger Bouillon Marise Lazaretti-Castro • Claudio Marcocci • Nicola Napoli8 • Rene Rizzoli9 • Andrea Giustina2 Division of Endocrinology, Escola Paulista de Medicina - Universidade Federal de Sao Paulo (EPM-UNIFESP), Sao Paulo, Brazil

item.title: On-line Vitamin D response simulation
item.description:
Personalise vitamin D 3 using physiologically based pharmacokinetic modelling

CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2021 May 7. doi: 10.1002/psp4.12640

Zhonghui Huang 1, Tao You 1

item.title: More than 16 reasons why Vitamin D trials fail
item.description:
• VitaminDWiki
      • Reasons for less benefit include:
    • Why do so many trials of vitamin D supplementation fail?
      • Abstract
      • Associations of vitamin D status with health outcomes
      • Vitamin D in evolution
      • Production of vitamin D
      • The common problem of vitamin D inadequacy
      • Route from inert vitamin D to hormonal activity
      • Induction of biological effects of vitamin D in target tissues
      • Many mechanistic effects improve function or reduce damage in non-skeletal tissues
      • Potential confounders of vitamin D RCTs
      • Design defects
      • Variations in 25(OH)D effect thresholds
      • Non-supplemental vitamin D intakes during RCTs and varying absorption
      • Other dietary factors modulating vitamin D efficacy
      • Stratification by vitamin D ‘status’ or by effect thresholds?
      • Factors affecting the consistency of 25(OH)D assay measurements
      • Serum 25(OH)D polymorphisms
      • Free vs bound 25-hydroxyvitamin D
      • Newer findings on disorders directly affecting serum 25(OH)D concentrations
      • Lack of long-term RCTs
      • Mendelian randomisation data
      • Future RCT design
      • Overview
      • References

VitaminDWiki

Reasons for less benefit include:

  1. Trials mistakenly use a single size dose for everyone

item.title: How I am reminded to take my Vitamin D once every 4 days
item.description:
I relabeled my pillbox

A visual reminder of when to take my vitamin D

If I happen to miss that day, I take them the next day or so.

Currently I am taking 2 capsules of 50,000 IU every 4 days

    Which is an average of 25,000 IU per day

Scores of studies have found that taking vitamin D infrequently is Better than Daily

    Unaware of a single study which has found daily to be better

item.title: Vitamin D less costly if give 100,000 IU monthly (but 50,000 IU biweekly should save more money)
item.description:
Can a Different Formulation of Vitamin D3 Allow Savings? An Analysis From an Italian Regional Perspective

Health Services Research and Managerial Epidemiology Volume 6: 1-8

Mario Sanò), Patrizia Dutto1, Stefano D’Anna1, and Carla Rognoni carla.rognoni@unibocconi.it

  1. ASL CN2 Alba-Bra, Farmacia Ospedaliera e Servizio Farmaceutico Territoriale

  2. Centre for Research on Health and Social Care Management (CERGAS), SDA Bocconi School of Management, Bocconi University, Milan, Italy

Vitamin D3 reported in Italy in 2017 the net expenditure of almost €180 million, reaching the first place for consumption and the third place for conventional pharmaceutical spending. The aim of the study was to evaluate whether a shift of vitamin D3 prescriptions toward 100 000 IU formulation, less costly, could allow savings from the health-care perspective. An approach promoting the prescription of this formulation has been applied in a local health authority (ASL CN2) in Piedmont Region (Italy) starting from 2015. The retail pharmaceutical market and the consumption of vitamin D3 has been analyzed from year 2014 to 2017 in order to evaluate differences in expenditures. Despite an increase in consumption, the introduction of the new formulation enabled ASL CN2 to save about €280 000 in 2017 considering the regional average expenditure per 1000 inhabitants as a reference. If Piedmont Region had presented an average expenditure in line with that of ASL CN2, the annual regional savings would have exceeded €7 million in 2017 alone. A shift of vitamin D3 prescriptions toward 100 000 IU formulations would allow reducing costs from the payer perspective. Savings may be used to contain public pharmaceutical expenditure or can be allocated to fund other health-care technologies and services.

item.title: Vitamin D RDA of 600 IU is not enough - global RCT meta-analysis
item.description:
A systematic review and meta-analysis of the response of serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D concentration to vitamin D supplementation from RCTs from around the globe

European Journal of Clinical Nutrition (2019)

Minjia Mo, Shijie Wang, Zun Chen, Xiamusiye Muyiduli, Shuojia Wang, Yu Shen, Bule Shao, Minchao Li, Danqing Chen, Zexin Chen & Yunxian Yu

This study accumulated a lot of RCT data for low, medium, and high doses, but apparently decided to not report on the medium and high doses because they could result in >50 ng/ml of vitamin D which they were told could be toxic, even though none of the studies found any evidence of toxicity

item.title: Monthly Vitamin D had a 20 percent better response than quarterly – small RCT
item.description:
The optimal dosage regimen of vitamin D supplementation for correcting deficiency in adolescents: a pilot randomized controlled trial.

Eur J Clin Nutr. 2018 Apr;72(4):534-540. doi: 10.1038/s41430-018-0098-x.

Wu F1, Xiao C1, Aitken D1, Jones G1, Winzenberg T2,3.

50,000 IU monthly vs 150,000 every 3 months

  • Monthly vitamin D dosing had higher response than 3 per month – RCT Jan 2018

item.title: Response to vitamin D increased 30 percent with Magnesium
item.description:
Grassroots Health unpublished data

Data read from chart
Magnesium 10,000 IU 20,000 IU
0 mg 62 ng 96 ng
600 mg 80 ng 120 ng
% increase 29% 25%

VitaminDWiki

Response

  • Overview Vitamin D Dose-Response

  • How you might double your response to vitamin D
